  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Air separator installed in the wrong location relative to the system pumps and boiler"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Relocate the air separator to the point of lowest solubility (typically on the suction side of the pump, downstream of the boiler) as per manufacturer manuals and hydronic design standards"
  ],
  "rationale": "An air separator installed in the wrong location cannot effectively remove entrained air from the hydronic system, which leads to air binding, reduced heat transfer efficiency, pump cavitation, and accelerated system corrosion. Hydronic standards require the air separator to be located where water temperature is highest and pressure is lowest (suction side of the pump).",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer Installation Manuals",
    "ASHRAE Systems and Equipment Handbook - Hydronic Heating and Cooling System Design"
  ]
}
